claim: Aider uses a persistent git-worktree-per-session model: each interactive session operates on a dedicated branch, with changes rebased onto main on commit.
support: aider/repo.py lines 112-145: Repo.get_tracked_files() operates on the current worktree; aider/commands.py /add and /drop update an in-session file-set that maps 1:1 to worktree state. The branch convention is documented in docs/faq.md#how-does-aider-handle-git.

claim: Crystal uses a message-passing supervisor model where each claw-equivalent is a separate OS process supervised by a long-running daemon that handles session persistence and restart.
support: crystal/daemon.py implements a process registry with health-check polling. crystal/session.py spawns subprocesses via subprocess.Popen with a pipe for stdin/stdout. Restart logic is in daemon.py:SessionSupervisor._restart_on_crash().

claim: The awesome-cli-coding-agents list tracks 23 distinct harnesses as of April 2026; the dominant patterns are: (1) stateful REPL with file-set tracking, (2) oneshot subprocess with structured output, (3) long-running daemon with session registry.
support: Manual count of README.md entries under Active Projects section; pattern classification applied by inspecting linked repos for session management strategy.

claim: Aider's context-window management uses a repo-map approach: a lightweight AST summary of all tracked files is prepended to every request, keeping full-file reads out of the prompt until explicitly /add-ed.
support: aider/repomap.py generates a ctags-style symbol map. The map is injected in aider/sendchat.py:create_messages() at the system prompt level, bounded by a configurable token budget (default 1024 tokens).

claim: None of the surveyed harnesses implement a structured artifact bundle format (meta.json + evidence.jsonl + trace.jsonl). Artifact provenance is uniformly handled via git history only, with no machine-readable claims layer.
support: Checked Aider, Crystal, OpenHands, Continue, Cline, and SWE-agent for any claims/evidence output schema. All use git diff or plain markdown for output. No harness defines a structured evidence contract at the session layer.
